commit.message:
Add setup command to install .NET global tool (#19)

* feat: add setup command to install .NET global tool

The plugin declares an MCP server that runs `roslyn-mcp`, but plugin
installation only clones the repo — it does not install the .NET global
tool. This leaves the MCP server unable to start after install.

Add a `/setup` slash command that guides users through installing
`RoslynMcp.Server` via `dotnet tool install -g`.

  is not blocked in Step 1
- Replace `roslyn-mcp --version` with `roslyn-mcp --help` since the CLI
  does not implement a --version flag

commit.message:
fix(service): RateLimit returns error instead of panicking on call without poll_ready

Previously, calling RateLimit::call() without a preceding
poll_ready() that returned Ready would decrement reserved_tokens
from 0 (wrapping or no-op depending on the path), then proceed to
invoke the inner service without holding a valid rate-limit token.
This violated the Service contract and could bypass rate limiting.

This commit makes call() check reserved_tokens == 0 upfront and
return RateLimitError::RateLimitExceeded immediately without touching
the inner service, matching the behavior callers expect from a
well-formed Service implementation.

Supporting changes to RateLimitFuture:
- Converted from a struct wrapping the inner future to an enum with
  two variants: Inner(F) for normal delegated futures, and
  Error(Option<RateLimitError<E>>) for immediate error responses.
- The type signature gains an E parameter: RateLimitFuture<F, E>.
- new() creates Inner variant, immediate_error() creates Error variant.
- Future::poll dispatches on variant: Inner polls the wrapped future,
  Error takes and returns the stored error.
- Debug impl shows the variant state.

Also fixes import ordering in load_shed.rs (Arc before AtomicBool).

Test: call_without_poll_ready_returns_rate_limit_exceeded verifies
that calling without poll_ready returns RateLimitExceeded without
invoking the inner service (uses PanicOnCallService), and that
token counts remain unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CFM-11: menu bar arrow key navigation and accessibility improvements (#410)

* feat: add arrow key navigation to menu bar with roving tabindex (CFM-11)

Implement the WAI toolbar pattern for the menu bar: a single tab stop
with ArrowLeft/ArrowRight navigation between items. Uses roving tabindex
so only the active item is in the tab order. Arrow keys are not
intercepted when the filename input is focused.

* refactor: make logo focus ring configurable via appFocusRingIcon (CFM-11)

Remove the hardcoded CODAP-specific LogoFocusRing SVG component. The
client application can now provide a custom focus ring image via the
appFocusRingIcon option. When not provided, a standard CSS focus outline
is used instead. Also uses appName for the logo button aria-label.

* fix: address accessibility audit findings for menu bar (CFM-11)

- Use data-toolbar-item attribute for precise toolbar button selection
  instead of broad 'button' query that could capture child components
- Add Home/End key support per WAI toolbar pattern recommendation
- Make toolbar aria-label translatable via tr()
- Change logo button label from descriptive to action-oriented (About)
- Add aria-label to filename input for screen readers
- Add useLayoutEffect dependency to reduce unnecessary DOM walks
- Add triggerProps to DropdownView for passing data attributes
- Add translation keys for toolbar label, about, and document name

* fix: address Copilot review feedback (CFM-11)

- Add items/otherMenus/languageMenu to useLayoutEffect deps so tabIndex
  syncs when toolbar contents change dynamically
- Use tr() for filename button aria-label instead of hardcoded English

commit.message:
refactor(web): make SessionLayer entropy source injectable for deterministic testing

The session middleware previously called getrandom directly via
generate_session_id(), making session ID generation non-deterministic
and untestable. This commit replaces the hardcoded OS entropy with a
pluggable EntropySource trait:

- generate_session_id_from_entropy(): replaces generate_session_id(),
  takes a &dyn EntropySource parameter. Uses Infallible error type
  since EntropySource::fill_bytes is infallible by contract.

- SessionLayer gains an `entropy: Arc<dyn EntropySource>` field,
  defaulting to OsEntropy (preserving production behavior). New
  builder method `entropy_source()` allows injection of custom sources.

- SessionMiddleware: stores the entropy Arc and passes it to
  generate_session_id_from_entropy when creating new sessions. Removes
  the internal_error closure and INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR_BODY constant
  since session ID generation can no longer fail.

- Debug impl for SessionLayer: now includes entropy.source_id() in
  debug output for observability.

Tests:
- FixedEntropy test helper: deterministic entropy source that cycles
  through a fixed 16-byte array, enabling reproducible session IDs.
- middleware_uses_injected_entropy_source_for_new_session_ids: verifies
  that a SessionLayer configured with FixedEntropy([0xab; 16]) produces
  the expected session ID "abababababababababababababababab".
- Existing tests updated to use generate_session_id_from_entropy(&OsEntropy).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(trace): make TraceMetadata deterministic by defaulting recorded_at to 0

TraceMetadata::new() previously called SystemTime::now() to populate
recorded_at, meaning two LabRuntime instances created with the same
seed would produce traces with different metadata, breaking the
deterministic replay guarantee that identical seeds yield identical
executions.

Changes:
- TraceMetadata::new(): recorded_at now defaults to 0 instead of
  capturing wall-clock time. This makes metadata a pure function of
  the seed, ensuring identical seeds produce byte-identical traces.
- TraceMetadata::recorded_at doc comment: updated to document that 0
  means "no wall-clock timestamp attached" and that deterministic
  runtime paths use 0 by default.
- metadata_creation_is_deterministic_for_same_seed (replay.rs test):
  new unit test asserting TraceMetadata::new(42) == TraceMetadata::new(42)
  and recorded_at == 0.
- replay_recording_metadata_is_stable_for_same_seed (lab/runtime.rs
  test): new integration test creating two LabRuntimes with seed 42
  and asserting their finished replay traces have identical metadata
  and events.
- metadata_new test: updated assertion to expect recorded_at == 0.

Callers that need wall-clock timestamps can set recorded_at explicitly
after construction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(service): LoadShed re-polls inner service on same-turn self-wake

When a Service::poll_ready implementation returns Pending but
immediately calls cx.waker().wake_by_ref() (a legitimate pattern for
services that need one more poll cycle to finalize readiness), the
LoadShed wrapper would observe only the initial Pending, set
overloaded=true, and start rejecting requests — even though the inner
service would return Ready on the very next poll.

This commit introduces a tracked probe waker mechanism:

- tracked_probe_waker(): creates a wrapper Waker that delegates to the
  real waker but also sets an AtomicBool flag when wake/wake_by_ref is
  called during the poll.

- poll_ready_preserving_self_wake(): polls the inner service with the
  tracked waker. If the result is Pending AND the waker was triggered
  during that same poll call, it immediately re-polls with the original
  caller's waker, catching services that self-wake synchronously.

LoadShed::poll_ready now uses poll_ready_preserving_self_wake instead
of calling self.inner.poll_ready directly.

Includes:
- SelfWakeReadyService test helper: returns Pending on first poll with
  an immediate self-wake, then Ready on second poll
- load_shed_preserves_same_turn_self_wake_readiness test: verifies
  LoadShed does not enter overloaded state and can call() successfully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
fix(scheduler): add explicit drop of local_ready_guard before acquiring local lock

Five cancel-path sites inline the remove-from-local-ready pattern but
held the local_ready mutex guard alive while acquiring the local
PriorityScheduler lock on the next line. Although the lock ordering
(local_ready before local) is correct and no deadlock was possible
with the current call graph, keeping the first lock held during the
second lock acquisition widens the critical section unnecessarily and
risks future deadlocks if additional code paths reverse the order.

This commit adds explicit `drop(local_ready_guard)` at each site:

- schedule_cancel_on_current_local: CURRENT_LOCAL_READY TLS path
- ThreeLaneScheduler::inject_cancel: pinned-worker cancel path
- ThreeLaneWorker::cancel_task_local: worker-local cancel
- ThreeLaneWorker::poll_task_loop: inline cancel during task polling
- ThreeLaneLocalCancelWaker::wake: cancel-waker promotion path

The local_ready guard is now dropped immediately after the
swap_remove, before the local scheduler lock is acquired. This
tightens the critical section and makes the sequential lock
acquisition pattern explicit at each call site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.9.5 — Security audit, integrity verification, supply chain hardening

Full security audit across all 6 crates, CI/CD, and dependencies.
All findings implemented:

Security hardening:
- Replace placeholder SHA-256 hashes with real HuggingFace LFS values
  for all 9 Whisper models — downloads are now integrity-verified
- Add 1-hour download timeout to reqwest model client
- Wayland socket detection: accept only actual sockets, not regular files
- UID fallback validation: reject non-numeric values
- Group membership parsing: trim whitespace from /etc/group entries

Supply chain hardening:
- Pin dtolnay/rust-toolchain and softprops/action-gh-release to
  full commit SHAs in CI and release workflows
- Pin flatpak-cargo-generator.py to specific commit with SHA-256
  checksum verification on download

Testing:
- Fix i18n locale test race condition by merging concurrent tests
  that mutate shared global LANGUAGE_LOADER into a single test

Website & release:
- Add Security Audit section to website with audit scope details
- Bump version to v0.9.5
- Add release notes to metainfo.xml

Audit result: zero unsafe blocks, zero backdoors, zero data exfiltration,
zero command injection vectors across 214 passing tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(types): correct Budget priority semiring from min to max

The Budget::meet combinator is a product semiring where each field uses
the "tighter" of two constraints. For deadline, poll_quota, and
cost_quota, "tighter" correctly means "min" (earlier deadline, fewer
allowed polls, lower cost cap). But for priority, the semantics were
inverted: higher numeric priority means more urgent work, so "tighter"
should be max (the more urgent of the two wins), not min.

This fix changes:

- Budget::meet: priority combination from .min() to .max()
- Budget::meet trace detection: "priority_tightened" now checks > instead
  of < since tightening means the combined priority exceeds either input
- Budget::INFINITE: priority from 255 to 0, making it the identity
  element for max (combining with INFINITE never raises priority)
- Budget::ZERO: priority from 0 to 255, making it the absorbing element
  (combining with ZERO always yields maximum urgency, matching the
  semantics of "zero resources but highest scheduling priority")
- Documentation comment: corrected "min (lower/tighter wins)" to
  "max (higher urgency wins)"
- All affected tests updated to match the corrected algebra:
  - test_infinite_budget: priority 255 → 0
  - test_zero_budget: priority 0 → 255
  - test_meet_basic: combined priority is now max(50, 100) = 100
  - test_meet_with_zero: combined priority is now max(200, 255) = 255
  - test_meet_with_infinite: priority 50 preserved (max(50, 0) = 50)
  - test_meet_identity_with_infinite: comment updated to explain
    INFINITE has priority 0 as identity for max

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
fix(database): add RAII guards to DbPool checkout/creation for panic safety

The checkout and fill_idle_connections paths both increment inner.total
before attempting potentially-panicking operations (is_valid, connect)
and only decrement on the error path. If the manager panics during
validation or connection creation, the total count leaks upward
permanently, eventually exhausting the pool's capacity with phantom
slots that hold no actual connection.

This commit introduces two scoped RAII guard types:

- ValidatingGuard: wraps the connection during is_valid() so that if
  the validation call panics, Drop automatically decrements total,
  bumps total_discards, and disconnects the connection. On the normal
  validation-failure path the connection is explicitly taken from the
  guard and cleaned up, preventing double-cleanup.

- PendingGuard: wraps the "total += 1" reservation during connect()
  so that if connect() panics, Drop decrements total. On both success
  and explicit error paths, the guard is disarmed (active = false)
  before the manual cleanup, preventing double-decrement.

The same PendingGuard pattern is applied in fill_idle_connections where
an identical total-leak risk existed.

No behavioral change on non-panicking paths. The guard types are
local to each method and zero-cost when not unwinding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(scheduler): inline remove_from_local_ready and simplify local waker dispatch

Eliminates three helper functions (`remove_from_local_ready`,
`remove_from_current_local_ready`, `try_local_priority_lanes`) by
inlining their logic at each call site. This removes unnecessary
abstraction layers and enables tighter lock scoping:

- `schedule_cancel_on_current_local`: now acquires the local_ready lock
  inline via CURRENT_LOCAL_READY TLS, keeping the critical section
  minimal and the lock ordering (local_ready before local) explicit at
  the call site rather than hidden behind a helper.

- `ThreeLaneScheduler::inject_cancel`: same inline pattern when
  cancelling a task pinned to a specific worker — lock, scan, remove,
  then move to cancel lane.

- `ThreeLaneWorker::cancel_task_local` and `poll_task_loop` cancel
  path: inline the remove-from-ready + move-to-cancel sequence,
  eliminating the function-call overhead on the hot worker loop.

- `ThreeLaneLocalWaker::schedule`: the ready-path no longer bounces
  through `schedule_local_task` (TLS lookup + lock) since the waker
  already holds a direct `Arc<LocalReadyQueue>` reference — just lock
  and push, saving a redundant TLS round-trip on every local wake.

- `ThreeLaneLocalCancelWaker::wake`: same inline treatment for the
  cancel-wake path.

Also simplifies a test assertion in `io_driver.rs`: the rearm wake-count
check now uses `> baseline` instead of `>= baseline + 1`, which is
semantically identical but clearer.

Net effect: -84 lines, fewer indirections on the scheduler hot path,
and lock ordering is visible at every call site instead of buried in
helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(optimizer): GPU-batched Bayesian optimizer stack

5 new files + backtester patch replacing 19-day grid search with
multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization:

- optimizer_prep.py: builds immutable CSR tensor context from bundle
  (bar_offsets + flat option arrays for O(1) per-bar chain slices)
- candidate_codec.py: SearchSpaceSpec / CandidateBatch encode/decode,
  Sobol/random/perturbation candidate generators
- optimizer_engine.py: K-candidate vectorised bar-major simulation,
  iron butterfly leg builder, mark-to-market via chain lookup,
  all 4 exit rules (SL / PT / max_dte_exit / hold_days)
- bayes_optimize_strategy.py: 3-phase BO driver (Sobol warmup →
  BoTorch GP+qLogNEI rounds → full-fidelity top-k eval),
  CSV checkpoint after each round, apply-best-config helper
- optimizer_parity.py: ParityReport comparing batch vs reference
  run_backtest() with documented tolerance for max_pos=1 simplification

backtester: add --optimize-mode {grid,bayes} + 6 --bo-* flags,
routing to run_bayes_optimizer() when bayes mode selected

Smoke test:
  python kaggle/condor_brain_backtest_v45.py --use-v43 \
    --strategies iron_butterfly --optimize --optimize-mode bayes \
    --bo-init-trials 8 --bo-batch-size 4 --bo-rounds 2

commit.message:
refactor(scheduler): interleave local and global lanes in ThreeLaneWorker dispatch

Restructure ThreeLaneWorker::next_task dispatch to interleave local
PriorityScheduler and global queue checks instead of processing all
global queues before local ones. The new two-phase approach:

Phase 1: Check the highest-priority global queue based on the
scheduling suggestion (timed for MeetDeadlines, cancel for default).

Phase 2: Acquire the local PriorityScheduler lock once and check
remaining cancel/timed lanes in strict suggestion order, interleaving
with global fallbacks.

This reduces latency for locally-queued high-priority tasks that were
previously blocked behind the full global queue scan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(io): stop read_line on definite UTF-8 errors even without newline

Fix process_fresh_chunk and process_pending_chunk to immediately
finish with an invalid-data error when the UTF-8 validation reports
a definite error (error_len().is_some()), even if no newline has been
found yet. Previously, the code only finished on newline, causing
indefinite reads when the stream contained malformed bytes that would
never produce a valid newline.

Three cases fixed:
- Fresh chunk with Utf8Error that has a definite error length
- Pending chunk with Utf8Error that has a definite error length
- Pending chunk where error_len is definite but no valid_len prefix

fix(service): load balancer re-polls backends that self-wake during probe

Fix LoadBalancer backend selection to handle services that become ready
on a follow-up poll after self-waking during the initial poll_ready
probe.

- Replace noop_waker in poll_service_ready_once with a tracked_probe_
  waker that records whether wake_by_ref was called during the probe.
- If a backend returns Pending but the waker was triggered (indicating
  the service became ready synchronously during the poll, e.g. after
  acquiring an internal semaphore), re-poll it once more before moving
  to the next candidate.
- This prevents skipping backends that are effectively ready but use a
  two-phase poll pattern (common with concurrency limiters and pools).

Includes WakeDuringPollReadyService test helper and associated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(service): rate limiter now checks inner service readiness before granting token

Rewrite RateLimit::poll_ready to check inner service readiness after
acquiring a token, rolling back the token if the inner service is not
ready. Previously, poll_ready_with_time only checked token availability
without consulting the inner service, which could lead to dispatching
requests to a service that wasn't ready to handle them.

Also:
- Generalize poll_ready_with_time to accept a Request type parameter,
  matching the Service trait's generic signature.
- Deduplicate the poll_ready implementation in Service::poll_ready by
  delegating to poll_ready_with_time, eliminating ~40 lines of
  copy-pasted refill/sleep logic.
- Add reserved_tokens tracking for token rollback on inner-service
  Pending/Err.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(database): add SleepFn hook to DbPool for deterministic testing

Extend the database connection pool with a configurable sleep hook
alongside the existing TimeGetter:

- Add SleepFn type alias (fn(Duration)) for synchronous retry/backoff
  loops that need to sleep outside async context.
- Add with_time_getter_and_sleep_fn constructor that accepts both hooks.
- Existing with_time_getter preserved as convenience (defaults sleep to
  thread::sleep).
- Add sleep_fn() accessor for downstream consumers.
- Type alias TimeGetter replaces raw fn() -> Instant throughout for
  clearer documentation.

This enables fully deterministic pool testing where both time reads
and sleep calls can be intercepted and controlled.

commit.message:
feat: collect commits from all branches for accurate merge ratio

Previously only default branch commits were collected, making merge ratio
always 100%. Now uses git log --all to collect from all branches and
git rev-list to determine actual default branch ancestry. Includes
deduplication for commits reachable from multiple branches.

Known limitation: squash merges replace original feature branch commits,
so abandoned branches with squash-merged commits may not be counted.

- scraperService: agrega "not_found" al tipo processingStatus y campo notFoundAt
- postal-tracking: muestra chip "No encontrado", opción en filtro, y alerta
  en el modal con la fecha en que se confirmó la inexistencia

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AGE Cypher queries: use exec_driver_sql to avoid bind param parsing

SQLAlchemy text() interprets [:MENTIONS] and [:CO_OCCURS] as bind
parameters. Switch all Cypher queries to _cypher() helper using
exec_driver_sql which bypasses parameter parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

portfolio: move Actions workflow to repo root .github/workflows

GitHub only detects workflows under <repo-root>/.github/workflows/.
Renamed to portfolio-publish.yml to distinguish from any future workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: scratch
repository.description: Random stuff I want to keep

commit.message:
fix: proxy API via Next.js rewrites to fix cross-origin login

Replace NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (build-time, unreliable) with Next.js
rewrites that proxy /api/* to BACKEND_URL at runtime. The browser
now calls relative /api/* on the same origin - no CORS, no build-time
embedding issues, no SameSite cookie problems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Saas-Gest-o-de-Igreja
repository.description: SIstema de Gestão de Igreja

commit.message:
fix: disable pointer-events on front card during swapPrev

swapPrev was disabling pointer-events on the back card (arriving),
but the cursor sticks to the front card (departing) which gets
demoted without firing mouseleave.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hotfix: fix API key encryption on save

save_config() silently stored plaintext keys when Fernet key was
unavailable. Now raises RuntimeError instead, preventing unencrypted
keys from being written to disk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.4.0: 11 new features, HarvestEstimate bugfix, flowering date auto-fill

New features: VPD Calculator, Trichome Harvest Tracker, Training Log,
Nutrient Deficiency Wizard, DLI Calculator, Batch/Run Manager,
Breeding/Pollen Tracker, pH/EC Calibration Reminders, Fleet View/Table
Mode, Grow Room Diagram, Harvest Cost Breakdown.

Bug fixes: HarvestEstimate now correctly checks m.growthStage (was m.stage),
flowering end date auto-fills when changing away from flowering stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: PlantManager
repository.description: Tropical Roots Maui - Plant Manager: Track mother plants, clones, care schedules, and grow room conditions. GPL-3.0

commit.message:
Change filamentId format to CF-prefixed 6-char hex; fix null density/diameter

- generateFilamentId now uses 16-bit mask and "CF%04X" format, producing
  a full 6-char tag ID (e.g. CF3A7F) — no "1" prefix needed
- Remove "1" prepend in WriteSpoolData; filamentId used as-is
- Default density/diameter to 1.24/1.75 in spool dialog when localData
  is absent or has zero values, preventing Spoolman 422 on filament POST

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Virginia transmission line data and scripts

Download 2,628 HIFLD transmission line segments for Virginia and inject
2,101 new lines (after dedup) into the dashboard map.

repository.name: datacenter-vulnerability
repository.description: US Data Center Infrastructure Vulnerability Dashboard — Power generation, transmission, and operator analysis for the top 10 DC markets

commit.message:
Design: align example scripts section with coder's implementation design

Updates Section 8 to match the coder's 4 fixture-based scripts
(diff-simple, diff-with-tables, diff-structural, diff-to-json)
instead of CLI tools. Adds smoke tests (EX-1 through EX-4) and
manual validation checklist. No network dependencies — all scripts
use committed HTML fixtures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: SageOx <ox@sageox.ai>

Add strategy tournament system, intraday EOD flatten, and README

- Tournament system: 6 specialized Claude Code agents compete across rounds
  with temporal split (IS 2003-2018, OOS 2019-2026), composite scoring,
  look-ahead bias detection, data access hooks, and per-tournament RULES.md
- Intraday mode: optional EOD position flatten with commission, ghost-trade
  prevention, daily-resolution warning, and shared force-close helper
- README: project overview, quick start, strategy examples, tournament docs
- Tests: 10 new tests covering EOD flatten (event-driven, vectorized,
  multi-symbol, brackets, commission, ghost trades, equity snapshots)
- Updated .gitignore to exclude tournament artifacts and local settings
